title: Ensure TCP/IP Connectivity
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on iSeries
text: Ensure TCP/IP Connectivity Follow the procedure to ensure a valid TCP/IP connection. Ensure that there is a TCP/IP port available for use by the Uniface Router. In this documentation, 13001 is assumed to be the port used. To restrict the TCP/IP port for use only by Uniface, reserve TCP/IP ports for Uniface: Enter GO CMDTCP and select Add TCP/IP Port Restriction . Set the range of restricted ports and the *TCP protocol. Set the user profile that owns the UROUTER job as the user profile. TCP/IP port assignment is optional and *IOSYSCFG authority is required to change the port assignment. Ensure that TCP services are running. Use the WRKTCPSTS command, specifying option 1, to verify that the TCP/IP interface is active. Ensure that your TCP/IP host and domain name are correctly configured. Use the CL command CHGTCPDMN to ensure the fields Host name and Domain name are filled correctly. Otherwise the Uniface Router and/or Uniface Server may not work as expected. Check that you have correctly specified at least one Domain name server on page 2 of CHGTCPDMN , or that you entered the host name in the local host table using ADDTCPHTE . Verify your domain name server or host table entry by checking that you can ping the iSeries system by its host name on the system itself. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
